---
title: "Model Overview"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Model Overview}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, echo = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

This vignette documents the two-strain dengue model, its state variables,
parameters, and the simulation workflow exposed by `twostraindengue`.

```{r}
library(twostraindengue)

parameters <- default_parameters()
state <- default_state(parameters)
time_grid <- seq(0, 2, by = 0.1)

model_output <- run_model(state, time_grid, parameters)
head(extract_reported_cases(model_output))
```

The package keeps the model interface deliberately small: defaults are exposed
through `default_parameters()` and `default_state()`, the ODE solve is wrapped
by `run_model()`, and reported classical and hemorrhagic cases can be extracted
with `extract_reported_cases()`.
